title: District South Bastar Dantewada Chhattisgarh MIS Assistant Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline
short_info: District South Bastar Dantewada Chhattisgarh has officially released the MIS Assistant Recruitment 2026 notification on 1 May 2026 on the official website www.dantewada.gov.in . A total of 1 vacancy has been announced through this recruitment. Candidates aged up to 40 years who have a Graduate degree (minimum 2nd class) and meet the eligibility criteria are eligible to apply for this position. The offline application process is open from 14 May 2026 and will remain open until 13 June 2026. Candidates can download the official notification PDF and submit their offline application using the dire

qualification.details: Graduate (minimum 2nd class) from a recognized university. Knowledge of Computer including MS Office. Minimum 2 years of experience in Forest Rights Act 2006 implementation in field areas. Chhattisgarh state domicile. Knowledge of tribal traditions, customs, and culture.

selection_process: Application Scrutiny Interview Merit List (50% Interview marks + 50% Graduation marks)
how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for District South Bastar Dantewada Chhattisgarh MIS Assistant Recruitment 2026 through offline mode should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Download the application form from the official website: www.dantewada.gov.in.
Fill the application form carefully with correct information.
Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates.
Check all details carefully before submission.
Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed.
Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 13-06-2026.
Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the MIS Assistant post?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the offline application for the MIS Assistant post is 13-06-2026. Candidates are advised to submit their applications well in advance to avoid any last-minute issues.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for this post?
faq.answer: Candidates must be Graduates (minimum 2nd class) from a recognized university. Additionally, knowledge of computers, including MS Office, is essential for this role.

faq.question: Q3. Is there any experience required for the MIS Assistant position?
faq.answer: Yes, a minimum of 2 years of experience in the implementation of the Forest Rights Act 2006 in field areas is mandatory for this position.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for the MIS Assistant recruitment?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for candidates applying for the MIS Assistant post is 40 years, as of 01-01-2026.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for the MIS Assistant post?
faq.answer: The selected candidate will receive a consolidated salary of Rs. 20000 per month for the MIS Assistant position.

faq.question: Q6. How will the selection be made for the MIS Assistant role?
faq.answer: The selection process involves application scrutiny, followed by an interview. A merit list will be prepared based on 50% of the interview marks and 50% of the graduation marks.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The official notification does not mention any specific application fee for this recruitment drive. Candidates should check the detailed advertisement for any updates.

faq.question: Q8. What is the application mode for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The application process for the MIS Assistant post is strictly offline. Candidates need to download the application form and submit it via post or as instructed in the notification.

faq.question: Q9. Does the candidate need to be a domicile of Chhattisgarh?
faq.answer: Yes, Chhattisgarh state domicile is a mandatory eligibility criterion for applying to the MIS Assistant post.

faq.question: Q10. What additional knowledge is preferred for this role?
faq.answer: Candidates with knowledge of tribal traditions, customs, and culture are preferred, which is beneficial for working in the Forest Rights Act implementation context.
